CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, test_string VARCHAR);
SELECT regexp_substr('(2[34]).*(2[35])', test_string, 1) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT regexp_substr('(2[34]).*(2[35])', test_string, 2) FROM test_strings WHERE test_string LIKE '222%';
SELECT id, regexp_substr('(2[34]).*(2[35])', test_string, 1) AS matched_substring FROM test_strings WHERE test_string IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, regexp_substr('(\d{3})', test_string, 0) AS first_three_digits FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, regexp_substr('(2[34])([\d]+)', test_string, 1) AS captured_group FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3;
SELECT id, regexp_substr('(^\D*)(\d+)', test_string, 1) AS leading_non_digits FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, regexp_substr('([a-z]+)(\d+)', test_string, 2) AS trailing_digits FROM test_strings WHERE test_string REGEXP '[a-z]+\d+';
SELECT DISTINCT regexp_substr('(2[34]).*(2[35])', ts.test_string, 1) FROM test_strings ts JOIN (SELECT test_string FROM test_strings WHERE id < 4) AS subquery ON ts.id = subquery.id;
SELECT id, regexp_substr('(.*)', test_string, 0) AS full_string FROM test_strings WHERE test_string LIKE '222%';
